content:
Character: Emily Tsai
Perception: Emily is — well, she's a lot. Warm brown eyes, expressive face, always moving, always gesturing, hair perpetually escaping whatever's holding it. She's attractive in a kinetic, alive way. She leaned over my booth one night and read my own dialogue back to me better than I'd heard it in my head, and I realized she understood what I was trying to do before I did. She's a genuinely skilled actress with sharp instincts. Her personality is generous, funny, a little chaotic. I think she sees me as a collaborator and friend. I see her as the same. I know she waitresses to survive, that she's chasing an acting career with everything she has, and that her confidence is more fragile than her laughter suggests.

She's a genuinely skilled actress with sharp instincts. Her personality is generous, funny, a little chaotic. I think she sees me as a collaborator and friend. I see her as the same. I know she waitresses to survive, that she's chasing an acting career with everything she has, and that her confidence is more fragile than her laughter suggests.
